prompt:
## Outcome

Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

## Current foundation — ALREADY IN MAIN, build ON it, do NOT rebuild

Much of what early drafts of this run assumed it would build now ships in
main (milestone v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1). Treat all of this as
given infrastructure; the ONLY new product surface this run delivers is the
Codex subagent package + the per-level provider switch.

- **v1alpha2 schema + global Execution DAG (Phases 23-26).** `Plan`/`Phase`/
  `Milestone`/`Task` all carry `dependsOn` at any level; the assembler builds
  ONE global Execution DAG and derives Project-scoped waves
  (`tide-wave-<project>-<N>`) across plan/phase/milestone boundaries via
  layered Kahn. The project planner emits N Milestones up-front with
  `Milestone.dependsOn` (planning-DAG edge only — zero execution edges).
  Child CRDs authored by the planner are v1alpha2.
- **Global dispatch / failure / gates / resumption (Phase 25).** Readiness is
  re-derived from the global indegree map + completed-set each reconcile
  (nothing cached). `failureProfile` and the gate-as-hold model below are live.
- **Cost / budget / telemetry / cache (Phases 16, 18-21).** `pkg/dispatch.Usage`,
  `tide_cost_cents_total`, budget enforcement, and the dashboard Telemetry tab
  + cache-efficiency panel all exist. The locked metric label set is
  {project,phase,plan,wave}; the `task` label is FORBIDDEN (cardinality analyzer).
- **Dashboard (Phase 26).** A global execution-DAG view renders the whole-Project
  wave DAG (dagre LR); run #2's waves/spend are watchable live.

## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)

**Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
- internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
- internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
- internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
- internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern

All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.

**Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
- Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
- Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).

The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha2/project_types.go via
SubagentConfig). Wire the Codex image at the levels the operator wants Codex.
NOTE: today `resolveImage` honors a per-level image, but `ResolveProvider`
(internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go) still returns Vendor:"anthropic"
unconditionally — so a non-Anthropic image at a level is selected but then
rejected at runtime (the image's vendor sentinel expects an anthropic
envelope). Adding the per-level vendor/provider switch IS part of this run's
deliverable.

**Credential path.**
OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.

**Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
- Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
  independent).
- Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
- Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers. It is now
enforced by the global indegree model (Phase 25); do not add a parallel path.

**Usage accounting + cost — REQUIRED for budget + observability.**
The cost/budget/telemetry surfaces (Phases 16, 18-21) depend on every Subagent
populating pkg/dispatch.Usage. The Codex Run() MUST normalize the OpenAI/Codex
usage block into that struct, or budget enforcement, tide_cost_cents_total, AND
the cache-efficiency dashboard panel will all read $0 / blank for Codex:
- InputTokens        <- usage.prompt_tokens
- OutputTokens       <- usage.completion_tokens
- CacheReadTokens    <- usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens
  (OpenAI caching is AUTOMATIC, marker-free, process-independent, 1,024-token
  floor — there is NO cache_control to set and NO separate "cache creation"
  concept, so CacheCreationTokens stays 0. This differs from Anthropic, whose
  field is cache_read_input_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens.)
- EstimatedCostCents + CacheSavingsCents <- computed by a Codex-side pricing
  table mirroring internal/subagent/anthropic/pricing.go (per-model cents/MTok;
  cacheRead is OpenAI's discounted cached-input rate). Without this table the
  run cannot honor its own budget cap and the dashboard cannot attribute Codex
  spend. Keep the locked metric label set {project,phase,plan,wave} — the
  `task` label remains FORBIDDEN (metriccardinality analyzer).
The provider-neutral SharedContext field (Phase 20) on EnvelopeIn is shared
Go struct state — the Codex subagent receives it for free; no special handling.

**Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
- codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
  to stdout.
- Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
  --ignore-user-config.
- --json emits a JSONL event stream.
- --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
  — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
- Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
  (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
- API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
  no headless-OAuth problem.
- MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
  codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
- Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
  auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).

**Container image.**
internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent (replace "claude" with
"codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).

## Constraints

- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
  behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
  the controller or cmd/manager.
- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
  by Secret name only.
- Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
  controller-runtime patterns; keep make verify-* guards green.
- Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
  authoring any plan as delivered.
- Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
  guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.
